question.text: When recovery is received from a segregated portfolio's impaired asset, the proceeds are:

answer.text: Donated to the Investor Protection Fund
Distributed proportionately to all unitholders who held units at the time of segregation
Added to the main portfolio's NAV for the benefit of current unitholders
Retained by the AMC as performance compensation

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Any recovery from the segregated portfolio's impaired asset is distributed proportionately to all unitholders who held units at the time of segregation. This ensures that the original investors who bore the impact of segregation receive any recovery. **The Trap:** The recovery does NOT go to the main portfolio or to current unitholders who may have joined after segregation. It goes specifically to those who were invested at the time of the credit event. **Cross-Exam Context:** This proportionate distribution mechanism is the key fairness principle behind side pocketing. It prevents wealth transfer between old and new investors. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Clients who held units during a segregation event should track recovery announcements. Even after redeeming their main portfolio units, they remain entitled to their share of any recovery from the segregated portion.
